elements.title: Bella Ramsey
elements.contents: ... that Bella Ramsey (pictured) is the youngest person ever to be nominated twice for a Primetime Emmy lead-actress award?

elements.title: Herero and Nama genocide
elements.contents: ... that German settler newspapers played a significant role in agitating for the Herero and Nama genocide?

elements.title: Kadiatu Kanneh-Mason
elements.contents: ... that British academic Kadiatu Kanneh-Mason quit her job to raise seven children and encouraged them to become musicians?

elements.title: Gallery of the Five Continents
elements.contents: ... that the artworks in the Louvre's Gallery of the Five Continents are meant to dialogue with each other?

elements.title: a 1998 referendum
elements.contents: ... that a 1998 referendum to repeal South Carolina's unenforceable interracial marriage ban was opposed by more than 38 percent of voters?

elements.title: Martín Ríos
elements.contents: ... that the mother of a man who was murdered by Martín Ríos said that the case opened a social debate in Argentina on the difference between psychosis and psychopathy?

elements.title: Palazzo delle Colonne
elements.contents: ... that the excavator of the Palazzo delle Colonne had to publish his findings from photographs and memory after World War II interrupted the excavation and vandals destroyed the records?

elements.title: Alexander F. Warley
elements.contents: ... that Confederate Navy officer Alexander F. Warley commanded the ironclad ram CSS Manassas to attack two ships on which he had previously served as a U.S. Navy sailor?

elements.title: Karrionic Hacktician
elements.contents: ... that some of the tracks on Karrionic Hacktician and Skin Stripper are only a few seconds long?

elements.title: Comic Beam
elements.contents: ... that Comic Beam has published manga making fun of its low sales?
